reason: speak_tts/stop_tts move to channel sends via State<TtsHandle>; the invoke contract must stay unchanged and follow IPC + Result error conventions.
The persistent TTS worker thread + managed Sender state is a backend structure change; keep lib.rs thin and follow Tauri state/threading conventions (Tts is !Send).
The speech queue core + cue types must be strictly typed (no any); injected clock/speaker deps typed.
Queue is unit-tested with fake timers + a mock speaker (DOM-free core); follow the testing conventions and keep coverage.
Verify the speak_tts/stop_tts invoke args stay aligned even though the Rust side gains a State param.
